title: Haunted Manor Material Restoration Studio
description: The user enters an abandoned manor scene where a broken glass window and a faded damask chair sit in disrepair. Using an interactive material editor, the user must restore both objects by adjusting PBR material properties — roughness, metalness, emissive glow, opacity, and environment map intensity — across multiple material zones to bring the scene back to its former glory.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES modules from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load GlassBrokenWindow.glb and ChairDamaskPurplegold.glb using GLTFLoader. Position the broken window upright at (0, 1.5, -2) and the chair at (0, 0, 1) facing the window, creating an abandoned manor vignette. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (3, 2.5, 5) looking at origin with OrbitControls. Add a dim PointLight (warm amber, intensity 2) at (2, 4, 3) simulating candlelight, a subtle blue HemisphereLight for moonlight ambiance, and an HDR-approximated environment using PMREMGenerator with a RoomEnvironment from three/addons. The floor should be a PlaneGeometry with a dark wood MeshStandardMaterial. Create a right-side HTML panel titled 'Material Restoration Studio' with these interactive controls: (1) A dropdown to select target object — Window Glass, Window Frame, Chair Fabric, Chair Wood. (2) A roughness slider (0-1, step 0.01). (3) A metalness slider (0-1, step 0.01). (4) An emissive color picker that sets emissive property on the selected material. (5) An opacity slider (0-1, step 0.01) that also toggles material.transparent. (6) An envMapIntensity slider (0-5, step 0.1). On load, traverse each GLB and categorize meshes into the four material zones by checking mesh names or material names. Store references in a map. When any slider or picker changes, apply the value to all meshes in the selected zone in real-time. Add a 'Validate Restoration' button that checks: window glass opacity must be between 0.15-0.4, window glass roughness below 0.15, chair fabric roughness above 0.7, chair fabric metalness below 0.1, chair wood metalness between 0.2-0.5, and at least one zone must have a non-black emissive color. Display a success banner with a golden glow CSS animation when all six conditions pass, or list which conditions still fail. Add a subtle particle system using Points with a BufferGeometry for floating dust motes illuminated by the candlelight.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  selectedMaterialIndex: number,         // index of currently selected material in dropdown (0=WindowGlass, 1=WindowFrame, 2=ChairFabric, 3=ChairWood)
  windowGlassRoughness: number,         // roughness value of window glass material (0-1)
  windowGlassOpacity: number,         // opacity value of window glass material (0-1)
  windowFrameMetalness: number,         // metalness value of window frame material (0-1)
  chairFabricRoughness: number,         // roughness value of chair fabric material (0-1)
  chairFabricEmissiveIntensity: number,         // emissive intensity of chair fabric (0-1 scale)
  chairWoodEnvMapIntensity: number,         // environment map intensity of chair wood material (0-5)
  totalSliderInteractions: number,         // cumulative count of all slider adjustments made
  hasRestoredWindow: boolean,         // true if window glass opacity > 0.8 and roughness < 0.3
  hasRestoredChair: boolean,         // true if chair fabric roughness < 0.5 and chair wood envMapIntensity > 2
  materialsFullyRestored: boolean,         // true when both window and chair meet restoration criteria
}
